title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 3353 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Temporal Coupling Decision.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-broker-fit-boundary-1vge8a" that uses case 3117 to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Broker Fit Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Broker Fit Boundary into observable performance evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Broker Fit Boundary, keep Broker Fit Boundary connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Broker Fit Boundary structural check is only one gate. a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Broker Fit Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Broker Fit Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Broker Fit Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab Go messaging team handles case 3696 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Broker Fit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely. In Go, adding a channel or broker wrapper does not remove temporal coupling.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 5315 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Keep the plausible Broker Fit Boundary output and hide the violated a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Broker Fit Boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Broker Fit Boundary happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Broker Fit Boundary for a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the transferred model · Lab Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Broker Fit Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab Go messaging team handles case 4410 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose HTTP, work distribution, pub/sub, or retained streams from coupling evidence; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
